free_text: A HIGH-FLUX REACTOR, DELIVERING A THERMAL
NEUTRON FLUX OF 5.0E14 NEUTRONS/CM2 AND SECOND ONTO
THE ILL RECOIL SPECTROMETER LOHENGRIN, WHERE
THE FISSION PRODUCTS ARE SELECTED IN FLIGHT
ACCORDING TO THE MAGNETIC AND ELECTRIC FIELD SETTINGS,
AN IONIZATION CHAMBER WAS USED TO DETECT THE

FORM OF OXIDE, PAINTED ON A 3 X 40 MM SURFACE OF A
20 X 90 MM TITANIUM BACKING. THE TARGET WAS COVERED
WITH A NI-FOIL OF 0.25 MICRO-M THICKNESS.
.THE DATA WERE CORRECTED FOR THE TARGET BURN-UP.
